title: The FaaS execution model
description: The platform creates, freezes, reuses, and reaps instances; your code runs as short-lived invocations on instances you do not control.
body: Use when reasoning about why warm/cold behavior and globals act as they do. The platform spins up an instance, runs init once, then handles invocations; between calls it may freeze the instance (background work pauses) and later reuse or destroy it. Module-scope state persists only while an instance stays warm, which is non-deterministic. Do not treat instance reuse as a guarantee. Actions: use module scope for connection reuse, external stores for durability. Validation signals include identical behavior across cold and warm runs. Risk boundary: assuming an instance (or its in-memory data) survives leads to lost state and cross-request leakage — design for ephemerality.
